items.headline: Applied Digital signs $7.5 billion AI data center lease with US hyperscaler
items.summary: Applied Digital has signed a long-term lease worth $7.5 billion with an unnamed U.S.-based hyperscaler at its new ‌Delta Forge 1 site, it said on Thursday, strengthening its ‌position as a data center provider for artificial intelligence workloads.  The 15-year lease covers 300 megawatts of computing capacity at the 430‑megawatt Delta Forge 1 site in southern U.S., the company said.  The deal makes the customer Applied Digital's second ‌U.S.-based investment‑grade hyperscale tenant ⁠across its data center sites and lifts total contracted lease revenue to more than $23 billion.

items.headline: Why Tesla investors should love this version of CEO Elon Musk
items.summary: A subdued, super-focused Musk is what Tesla investors need in what is arguably the most important moment for the company in years.

items.headline: GLD Has Returned ~9% Year to Date While the S&P 500 Has Stalled. Here Is What Is Driving Gold Higher
items.summary: Gold’s performance over the last few years is a good reminder that stocks and bonds alone are not enough for diversification, especially after 2022. Owning equities across all 11 sectors and across different geographic helps, but it still leaves you exposed to the same underlying risk factors. At some point, you want to add assets ... items.headline: This Week In Energy Transition - Europe's Path to Energy Independence by 2050
items.summary: A new report by Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) has highlighted the potential for Europe to reduce its reliance on imported fossil fuels by up to 80% by 2050, through accelerated electrification and a shift towards renewable energy. The analysis underscores the geopolitical and financial benefits of this transition, as the region currently spends approximately EUR 250 billion annually on fossil fuel imports, which represents about 40% of its energy demand. items.summary: United CEO Scott Kirby floated the idea of a merger with American to a Trump administration official earlier this year, according to sources.

items.headline: More people are turning to AI to help navigate tight finances
items.summary: Americans expect inflation and everyday costs to be major factors in their financial well-being this year, and more are counting on artificial intelligence to help them navigate.

items.headline: 3 Dividend Stocks To Consider Yielding Over 3.2%
items.summary: The market has climbed 1.7% in the last 7 days and is up 34% over the past 12 months, with earnings forecast to grow by 16% annually. In this thriving environment, dividend stocks yielding over 3.2% can offer investors a combination of income and potential capital appreciation, making them an attractive consideration for those seeking stability and growth.
